name: Celebration entries beyond per-batch cap are marked seen and permanently dropped
description: [FCR 2026-06-21 · L1-01] use-celebration adds every unseen entry's key to seenQueueKeysRef during the filter pass, but only the first `remaining` (cap 2) are queued; entries past the cap are marked seen yet never shown, so a batch of 3+ celebrations silently loses the 3rd onward. Fix: add key to seenQueueKeysRef only for entries that land in toShow.
acceptanceCriteria: Root cause: use-celebration marks every unseen queue entry as seen during filtering before applying MAX_TOASTS_PER_BATCH, so entries beyond the per-batch cap are excluded from display later even though they were never queued. Variants: (1) batch of exactly 2 celebrations still queues both; (2) batch of 3+ queues only the first capped entries and leaves overflow eligible for a later pass instead of marking them seen; (3) celebrationLevel filtering must not mark filtered-out entries as seen prematurely. Regression test: apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-celebration.test.tsx adds or updates a batch-over-cap case that fails when seenQueueKeysRef.add runs before toShow selection and passes when only displayed entries are marked seen.
